title: Bristol Myers details Phase 3 trial win for mezigdomide, its next-generation blo
headline: Bristol Myers details Phase 3 trial win for mezigdomide, its next-generation blood cancer drug positioned as a Pomalyst successor.
key_facts: - Phase 3 trial successful (specific endpoints and patient numbers not available in fetched text) - Mezigdomide is a CELMoD (cereblon E3 ligase modulator), next-gen successor to Pomalyst (pomalidomide) - Story published ~11 hours before fetch (approx. early morning May 29)

title: ASCO 2026 Annual Meeting opens May 29–June 2 in Chicago, with Merck presenting d
headline: ASCO 2026 Annual Meeting opens May 29–June 2 in Chicago, with Merck presenting data for a cancer drug licensed from a China-based biotech that it calls a "cornerstone."
key_facts: - Meeting dates: May 29–June 2, 2026, McCormick Place, Chicago - Merck presenting study results giving "greater conviction" therapy was worth placing into a "sprawling Phase 3 program" - Drug licensed from China-based biotech (name not specified in fetched headline)

title: Agios Pharmaceuticals stops development of tebapivat in low-risk myelodysplastic
headline: Agios Pharmaceuticals stops development of tebapivat in low-risk myelodysplastic syndromes (MDS) after subpar results.
key_facts: - Tebapivat: pyruvate kinase activator - Failed a "key test" per BioPharma Dive roundup - Development ended, not paused

title: Replimune asks FDA for re-review of its melanoma drug after agency stood by past
headline: Replimune asks FDA for re-review of its melanoma drug after agency stood by past rejections.
key_facts: - Third attempt at approval ("third swing" per BioPharma Dive) - Company describes FDA talks as "productive" - FDA has previously rejected the drug (at least twice)
